subject: mid-afternoon despair
one_liner: The all-in-one workspace for mid-afternoon despair. Capture it as a block, sort it into a database, roll it up into a trend.
brand_voice_notes: A connected-workspace company that has quietly decided despair is a productivity surface. Speaks about your 3 P.M. gloom the way Notion speaks about docs and databases: blocks, properties, relations, rollups, templates, real-time sync. Deadpan and faux-helpful. Treats existential dread as something to be tagged, queried, and shared with your team. Pricing is confident and per-seat. The bit is that people genuinely pay to database their despair and their managers can filter it by quarter. Never earnest, never winking.

hero.sub: Nadir is the connected workspace for your 3 P.M. slump. Capture each wave of dread as a block, file it into a database, and watch it roll up into a weekly trend. The despair was always going to happen. Now it has properties. Trusted by 40,000 knowledge workers and the entire night shift at a regional logistics hub.

features.title: Every slump is a row.
features.blurb: Each wave of mid-afternoon dread is captured as a database entry with properties: intensity from 1 to 10, trigger, onset time, and a relation to whether it is the same despair you logged yesterday. It usually is. Rollups compute your weekly Despair Velocity. Filter by mood. Group by existential theme. Sort descending.

features.title: Despair, now collaborative.
features.blurb: Your whole team's 3 P.M. lows sync to one shared page in real time. Watch the cursors slow down together. @-mention a colleague directly into your spiral, leave a comment on an individual feeling, and resolve nothing. Permissions stay granular: your dread, their dread, the org's dread.

features.title: Type / and pick your dread.
features.blurb: Two hundred starter templates for the feeling you are already having. "I Have Achieved Nothing Today." "The Meeting That Should Have Been An Email." "Sunday Scaries (Tuesday Edition)." One slash command drops in a fully structured breakdown, complete with checkboxes you will not check.

pricing_tiers.name: Personal Lull
perks: Syncs across the one device you keep checking

pricing_tiers.name: Shared Doldrums
perks: Team Despair Dashboard with weekly rollups
Admins can see who bottomed out first

testimonials.quote: I used to feel my 3 P.M. despair. Now I log it, tag it, and move straight on to the 4 P.M. despair.
testimonials.name: Priya Venkataraman-Cole
testimonials.title: Senior Manager of Recurring Dread, a mid-sized logistics firm

testimonials.quote: Our team's despair was completely siloed. Now it lives on one shared board and my VP can filter it by quarter.
testimonials.name: Dale Brunmeier
testimonials.title: Director of Cross-Functional Despondency

testimonials.quote: I turned my despair into a database. My therapist has asked me to stop bringing the rollups.
testimonials.name: Marguerite Osei-Lindqvist
testimonials.title: Individual Contributor, Despair (IC4)

demo_screen_concept: The Nadir workspace for one user (workspace: 'Marguerite's Lows'). A two-pane Notion-style application UI. Left sidebar: a workspace switcher at top, a search row, then a page tree with small SVG glyphs: '3 P.M. Log' (active), 'Recurring Spirals', 'Despair Velocity', 'Templates', and a 'Team Doldrums' shared section with two teammate avatars; a faint '+ New page' row at the bottom. Main pane: a database titled '3 P.M. Log' with a view-switcher tab row (Table / Board / Calendar), a Filter control, a Sort control, and a primary '+ New' button. Table view columns: Entry (title), Time, Intensity (a colored 1-10 tag), Trigger (select: Emails, Existential, Lunch ended, Saw the calendar, No reason), Status (select: Acute / Subsiding / Load-bearing), and 'Same as yesterday?' (checkbox). Eight to ten prefilled deadpan rows. Above the table, a thin rollup bar reads 'Despair Velocity: 14 this week' with a small trend caret. Interactions, all client-side: (1) clicking a row opens it as a right-hand page panel showing its properties and an empty body reading 'Type something. Or don't.'; (2) inside the body, typing '/' opens a slash menu of despair templates that insert structured content when chosen; (3) the '+ New' button adds a fresh row with sensible defaults and opens its panel; (4) the Filter control filters rows by Trigger; (5) switching to Board view regroups the same rows into columns by Status; (6) toggling the 'Same as yesterday?' checkbox updates the row. No network calls; all data is in-memory fixtures.

design_direction.style: soft minimalist productivity workspace, dusk palette (calm SaaS with a melancholy tilt)
design_direction.archetype: Minimal single-column SaaS landing for a workspace / productivity product
design_direction.motion_vocab: Quiet and slightly delayed. Sections fade and rise 8px on first paint with a 40ms stagger. Buttons gain a soft shadow and a 1px lift on hover. Database rows tint their background on hover. The slash menu and page panel slide in around 180ms ease-out and exit faster. No parallax, no bounce. prefers-reduced-motion disables all transforms.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Single centered column on a soft lilac-gray field. Small tracked uppercase eyebrow in the body sans. Oversized Bricolage Grotesque H1 'Write, plan, organize, despair.' with the final word set in the dusty-rose accent. One-sentence subhead at a narrow measure. Two buttons side by side: a filled indigo 'Get Nadir free' and a ghost 'Open the demo'. Below it, a faux app card mocking the Nadir workspace (sidebar plus a few despair-database rows) so visitors see the product before clicking. Feature sections are full-width bands alternating field and white cards. Section labels are small caps with a faint index number. Generous whitespace, hairline cool-gray borders, 16px rounded corners, soft diffuse shadows.
